steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined sentence is: "Now the house is a museum connected to Hawthorne and other literary figures." It follows a sentence that identifies the Old Manse as Hawthorne's former home and connects the house to his writing. We need to identify what role this sentence plays—what job it does in the paragraph's structure.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It summarizes the career of an author mentioned in the previous sentence.: The sentence says nothing about Hawthorne's career. It describes what the building is now used for, not what Hawthorne accomplished. ✗
  • Choice B — It states the current purpose of a building mentioned in the previous sentence.: Exactly right. "Now the house is a museum" shifts from the building's past to its present function as a museum. ✓
  • Choice C — It recommends the works of an author mentioned in the previous sentence.: The sentence makes no recommendation about Hawthorne's writing. It simply describes the building's present use. ✗
  • Choice D — It recounts the history of a place mentioned in the previous sentence.: The previous sentence already provided historical context about the house. The underlined sentence pivots to the present with "Now"—it is not a historical recount. ✗
That matches Choice B — It states the current purpose of a building mentioned in the previous sentence..
